forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over southeastern SA will remain dominant as it slowly moves east and into the Tasman Sea on Friday. A cold front move across across South Australia over the weekend. A low pressure system develops and lingers near the southeast early next week whilst a ridge high pressure builds over the west.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches about the east and southeast. Isolated showers developing about the far west during the day. Maximum temperatures near to below average. East to northeasterly winds, fresh at times about the west.
Dry and partly cloudy. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Isolated showers in the west, possibly reaching central parts in the evening. Showers becoming scattered about western coasts during the afternoon. Possible thunderstorms about the far west. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, with a south to southwesterly change moving across western districts during the day, fresh at times near the coast.

Isolated showers extending statewide, tending scattered about the southern agricultural area and western coasts. Maximum temperatures below average. North to northwesterly winds, ahead of a south to southwesterly change about the west moving across remaining districts during the day, fresh at times near the coast.

text.p: Isolated showers about the agricultural area and south of the pastoral districts, becoming scattered about windward coasts and ranges. Isolated showers clearing from the north. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds fresh at times about the coast.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: less than 2 mm about the far northeast, 1 to 5 mm about the northern agricultural area and south of the pastoral districts, increasing to 5 to 15 mm about western coasts and the southern agricultural area.
